articles.title: Peace on Pause: Israel and Lebanon Extend Ceasefire, but Hezbollah Says No
articles.summary: What happens when two countries agree to stop fighting, but a powerful group inside one of them refuses to go along? That's the drama unfolding right now. Israel and Lebanon have extended their ceasefire by three weeks after talks at the White House. President Trump helped broker the deal, but Hezbollah — the Iranian-backed militant group — says it won't follow any agreement. Meanwhile, a Lebanese journalist was killed by an Israeli strike, sparking outrage. Can this fragile pause lead to lasting peace?

articles.title: No Jokes This Year: A Mentalist Will Try to Read Trump's Mind at the White House Dinner
articles.summary: Forget the roast — this year's White House Correspondents' Dinner is swapping comedians for a mentalist. Oz Pearlman, famous for guessing strangers' PIN numbers on social media, will perform in front of President Trump and hundreds of journalists. Pearlman says he's been planning his act for a decade and that reading Trump's mind is "the most impressive thing you could ever do." Can he pull it off?

articles.title: Iran Fires Back at Trump: 'We Are One Soul, Not a Land of Rifts'
articles.summary: President Trump has been claiming that Iran's leaders are locked in a bitter internal feud. But Iran's top officials just issued a rare, coordinated response: the president, parliament speaker, and national security council all posted the exact same message declaring unity. "In Iran, there are no radicals or moderates," they said. Meanwhile, Trump threatened to "shoot and kill" anyone laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. Is Iran really united — or is this a show of strength?
